things.name: Osaka Castle & Museum
things.description: 16th-century castle with a superb 8-floor museum inside — cherry blossoms and moats make it magical in spring

things.name: Dotonbori
things.description: Osaka's neon-lit entertainment strip — the giant Glico man, endless takoyaki stalls and canal boat rides

things.name: Universal Studios Japan
things.description: Japan's most visited theme park — The Wizarding World of Harry Potter and Super Nintendo World

things.name: Nara Day Trip
things.description: Ancient capital with 1,200 wild sika deer roaming freely alongside the enormous Todai-ji Buddha hall

things.name: Kuromon Ichiba Market
things.description: Osaka's kitchen — 180 stalls of fresh seafood, wagyu and street-food heaven since 1902

things.name: Shinsekai & Tsutenkaku Tower
things.description: Retro early 20th-century neighbourhood with the 1956 Tsutenkaku tower and legendary kushikatsu restaurants

things.name: Kyoto Day Trip
things.description: Japan's cultural capital just 15 minutes by Shinkansen — Fushimi Inari, Arashiyama bamboo and Geisha district

things.name: Osaka Aquarium Kaiyukan
things.description: One of the world's largest aquariums — whale sharks, manta rays in a 5.4-million litre Pacific Ocean tank

things.name: Sumiyoshi Taisha Shrine
things.description: One of Japan's oldest shrines (211 AD) in its own unique architectural style predating Chinese Buddhist influence

things.name: Umeda Sky Building
things.description: Two towers connected by a floating garden observatory 173 m up — particularly spectacular at night
